title: Agent Pulse
description: Source-code motion for curated AI-agent and agent-adjacent projects covered by The Git Reporter.

scope.coverageStatement: V0.1 measures visible source-code motion, hardening-subject proxy signals, daily git trends, and The Git Reporter article coverage only. Community feedback metrics are absent until the GitHub feedback collector ships.

editorialRead.headline: Agent Pulse — July 6, 2026: code motion is visible; community feedback is not collected yet.
editorialRead.readHeadline: What the watched source shelf says today
editorialRead.oneLineSummary: 74 commits landed across 4 active watched projects today; openclaw/openclaw is the busiest daily signal while openai/codex carries the deepest coverage trail.
editorialRead.dek: Today Agent Pulse tracks 10 watched projects through public git motion, source receipts, and The Git Reporter coverage. Treat it as a reported signal board, not a definitive market ranking.
bullets: openclaw/openclaw is still the loudest source-code motion signal with 1,649 commits in seven days.
NousResearch/hermes-agent is the strongest baseline mover, 132.8 commits above its current 30-day pace.
openclaw/openclaw leads the hardening-subject proxy, built from commit-subject evidence rather than release quality.
openai/codex has the deepest article trail on The Git Reporter, with 65 linked articles.
openai/codex cooled the most week-over-week, down 193 commits from the previous seven days.
editorialRead.watchNext: The next meaningful upgrade is feedback flow: issues opened and closed, review latency, stale backlog, and the share of work that starts outside the maintainer team.
editorialRead.caveat: V0.1 does not collect GitHub issue flow, PR review timing, Discord, X/Twitter, Reddit, Hacker News, or private backlog data.
